meta.title: Daily Reflection Tree
meta.description: A deterministic end-of-day reflection tool covering three psychological axes: Locus of Control, Contribution Orientation, and Radius of Concern.

axes.axis2: Orientation — Entitlement vs Contribution (Campbell et al., 2004; Organ, 1988)

nodes.text: Good evening. Before you close the day, let's spend five minutes with it. No right answers — just honest ones.
How would you describe today in one word?
options: Productive
Challenging
Frustrating
Mixed
answer=Productive|Mixed:A1_Q_AGENCY_HIGH
answer=Challenging|Frustrating:A1_Q_AGENCY_LOW

nodes.text: You said '{A1_OPEN.answer}'. When something went well today, what drove it?
options: I had prepared well
I adapted when things shifted
Good timing — things just lined up
The team carried it

nodes.text: You said '{A1_OPEN.answer}'. When it got hard, what was your first instinct?
options: Find something I could still control
Wait to see what others would do
Push through alone
Feel stuck and frustrated
answer=I had prepared well|I adapted when things shifted:A1_Q2_INTERNAL
answer=Good timing — things just lined up|The team carried it:A1_Q2_EXTERNAL
answer=Find something I could still control|Push through alone:A1_Q2_INTERNAL
answer=Wait to see what others would do|Feel stuck and frustrated:A1_Q2_EXTERNAL

nodes.text: When something didn't go as planned today, where did your mind go first?
options: What can I change next time?
What did I miss that I could have caught?
Who else was responsible for this?
Why does this keep happening to me?

nodes.text: Think about a moment today when you felt out of control. What would have made it different?
options: If I had acted sooner
If someone else had done their part
If the situation had been fairer
If I had clearer information
answer=What can I change next time?|What did I miss that I could have caught?:A1_R_INT
answer=Who else was responsible for this?|Why does this keep happening to me?:A1_R_EXT
answer=If I had acted sooner|If I had clearer information:A1_R_INT
answer=If someone else had done their part|If the situation had been fairer:A1_R_EXT

nodes.text: You see your hand in what happened today — not in a self-blaming way, but in a 'I was a factor' way. That's agency. It doesn't mean everything was in your control. It means you stayed curious about the parts that were.
A tough day pulls attention outward — to circumstances, to others, to what was unfair. Fair enough. But somewhere in there, you made at least one call. Even a small one. It's worth finding it.
Now let's shift. You've looked at how you responded to today. Let's look at what you gave.
Think about your interactions today. Which of these best describes a moment?
options: I helped someone who didn't ask
I felt I deserved more recognition than I got
I did something no one will probably notice
I felt frustrated that others weren't contributing equally
answer=I helped someone who didn't ask|I did something no one will probably notice:A2_Q_CONTRIB
answer=I felt I deserved more recognition than I got|I felt frustrated that others weren't contributing equally:A2_Q_ENTITLE

nodes.text: What was it that made you give that extra effort today?
options: Someone needed it and I could help
It was the right thing for the project
I knew no one else would step in
It felt good to contribute

nodes.text: When you felt underrecognized today, what would have made it right?
options: A simple acknowledgment from someone
Credit for my work in front of others
A tangible reward or outcome
Someone else doing more of the work

nodes.text: Looking back at today, what were you thinking about more?
options: What I could contribute or build
Whether my effort was being fairly measured
How the team was doing overall
Whether I was getting what I need to grow

nodes.text: Is there something you gave today that surprised even you?
options: Yes — I stepped up when I didn't expect to
A little — I did more than required
Not really — I stuck to what was expected
No — today was about receiving, not giving
answer=What I could contribute or build|How the team was doing overall:A2_R_CONTRIB
answer=Whether my effort was being fairly measured|Whether I was getting what I need to grow:A2_R_ENTITLE
answer=Yes — I stepped up when I didn't expect to|A little — I did more than required:A2_R_CONTRIB
answer=Not really — I stuck to what was expected|No — today was about receiving, not giving:A2_R_ENTITLE

nodes.text: You leaned into giving today — even when it wasn't required. That's not small. Discretionary effort is what teams are actually built from. It rarely shows up in metrics, but it always shows up in culture.
There's nothing wrong with wanting recognition — it's human. But notice where your energy went today: toward what you received, or toward what you gave. Tomorrow is a new data point.
One more lens. You've reflected on your agency and what you gave. Now — how wide was your circle of concern today?
When you think about today's biggest challenge, who came to mind?
options: Just me — it was my problem to solve
My team — we were all in it together
A specific colleague who had it harder than me
The people we serve — customers or end users
answer=Just me — it was my problem to solve:A3_Q_SELF
answer=My team — we were all in it together|A specific colleague who had it harder than me|The people we serve — customers or end users:A3_Q_OTHER

nodes.text: When you were deep in your own challenge today, was there a moment you noticed someone else struggling?
options: Yes, and I did something about it
Yes, but I was too focused on my own plate
No — I didn't have bandwidth to look up
No — everyone seemed fine

nodes.text: When you thought about others today, what did you actually do with that awareness?
options: I took an action to help
I checked in to understand their situation better
I acknowledged it mentally but kept to my own work
I shared their perspective with someone who could help
answer=Yes, and I did something about it:A3_R_ALTROCENTRIC
answer=Yes, but I was too focused on my own plate|No — I didn't have bandwidth to look up|No — everyone seemed fine:A3_R_SELF
answer=I took an action to help|I checked in to understand their situation better|I shared their perspective with someone who could help:A3_R_ALTROCENTRIC
answer=I acknowledged it mentally but kept to my own work:A3_R_SELF

nodes.text: You moved beyond your own frame today — even briefly. Maslow called this self-transcendence: not self-erasure, but the expansion of your circle of concern. It's both a skill and a practice. You practiced it today.

Today your energy stayed close. That's not a failure — it's often necessary. But notice: when we're absorbed in our own frame, we miss the wider story. Tomorrow, try one moment of looking up. It doesn't have to be grand.

Today's reflection:

Axis 1 (Agency): You leaned {axis1.dominant}.
Axis 2 (Contribution): You leaned {axis2.dominant}.
Axis 3 (Radius): You leaned {axis3.dominant}.

You described today as '{A1_OPEN.answer}'. {summary_reflection}

summary_templates.internal_contribution_altrocentric: You showed up with agency, generosity, and a wide gaze today. That's a rare combination on a hard day. Notice it.
summary_templates.internal_contribution_self: You were purposeful and generous today — and that's meaningful. Tomorrow, try widening your view to include one more person's experience.
summary_templates.internal_entitlement_altrocentric: You held your agency and cared about others — a strong foundation. The contribution layer will come as you redirect that self-awareness outward.
summary_templates.internal_entitlement_self: Your agency is your asset. The next step is asking: what can this capacity *give*?
summary_templates.external_contribution_altrocentric: Even when today felt out of control, you gave generously and noticed others. That's integrity under pressure.
summary_templates.external_contribution_self: You gave today, and that matters. The locus piece is worth sitting with — where *did* you have a choice, even a small one?
summary_templates.external_entitlement_self: Today was hard, and it showed. That's okay. But both agency and generosity are choices available tomorrow. One small shift is enough to start.
summary_templates.external_entitlement_altrocentric: You cared about others even when you were struggling — that generosity of spirit is worth honoring. Tomorrow, look for one moment where you were the agent.

nodes.text: Rest well. See you tomorrow.
